topic: An athlete doesn't make the team they expected to join
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the locker room hummed with an irritating persistence. Liam slumped onto the bench, the vinyl cool and unforgiving beneath him. He stared at the team roster taped to the wall, his jaw tight. He’d poured every ounce of sweat, every early morning drill, into this season, only to see his name conspicuously absent from the final selection. A low, almost inaudible sigh escaped his lips, a sound heavy with disappointment. "Right," he muttered to himself, picking up his worn duffel bag with unnecessary force. "Guess the universe has other plans." He shoved his hands deep into his pockets, the tension radiating off him like heat.

[story 2]

"Unbelievable," Sarah breathed, shaking her head as she watched the final names being called. Mark stood by the doorway, arms crossed, his gaze fixed on the polished floor tiles. He felt a knot tightening in his chest, a dull, heavy pressure that made breathing feel shallow. He didn't look up when the coach finished, his expression utterly flat. When the announcement was over, Mark simply turned and walked toward the exit, his steps measured and slow, as if every movement required immense effort. He didn't say anything, just let the silence swallow him whole.

[story 3]

The weight of the discarded water bottle felt substantial in Maya’s hand. She traced the condensation with a fingertip, her eyes scanning the faces of the teammates who were now celebrating, their laughter bright and sharp. A deep furrow settled between her brows, pulling the skin taut across her forehead. She didn't offer a word of congratulations; instead, she methodically began packing her gear, each zip pulled shut with a deliberate, almost aggressive motion. "Fine," she mumbled, the word sandpaper rough in her throat. "This is just... inconvenient."
